qa_pairs.question: How can one display the command-line options that the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) is using to run a Java program?
qa_pairs.answer: You can display all the command-line options the JVM is going to run with by specifying the `-XX:+PrintCommandLineFlags` flag when executing your Java program, for example: `java -Xms256m -Xmx4g -XX:+PrintCommandLineFlags -version`.

qa_pairs.question: What is an alternative method to setting Java Virtual Machine (JVM) run-time options besides passing them on the command line?
qa_pairs.answer: Alternatively, you can use the `JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS` environment variable to set JVM run-time options. For example: `export JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS="-Xms256m -Xmx2g"`.

qa_pairs.question: When is it particularly convenient to use the `JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS` environment variable for setting Java run-time options?
qa_pairs.answer: Using the `JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS` environment variable is especially convenient if you launch multiple Java calls or if a Java program is called from another Java program, as it eliminates the need to pass options repeatedly on the command line.
